statement.markdown: Given a trie data structure, implement a method to count the number of nodes in the trie. Note that leaf nodes are also counted. However, for each node, you must return the count of its children that actually exist in the trie.
statement.inputFormat: A trie is implemented as a map where a key represents a string and a value is a map of its children.
statement.outputFormat: A list of integers where the index at position i represents the count of children of the node at position i.

examples.explanation: The trie contains the word 'apple'. The root node has two children: 'a' and 'p'. Both exist in the trie.

hints.text: Start at the root of the trie and recursively explore each subtree to keep track of the node count and children count.
When a node is a leaf node, it has no children, so the count should be 0.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Recursively count the nodes of the trie, keeping track of the children count of each node.
editorial.optimalSolution: To count the children of a node, iterate over all keys of its value map.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the approach is guaranteed by the proper counting of the children for each node.
